news.title: Allahabad HC upholds land acquisition for Noida airport expansion, stresses rehabilitation measures
news.para: The Allahabad High Court has green-lighted the land acquisition necessary for expanding the Noida International Airport. The court dismissed objections from local landowners, affirming that the acquisition adhered to the 2013 land law. Importantly, it mandated comprehensive rehabilitation for existing residential land occupants before any possession occurs, paving the way for the airport's ambitious growth plans.

news.title: Chennai civic body enables QR code to fast-track property tax payments
news.para: Greater Chennai Corporation has introduced QR code payments for property tax to expedite collections following poll-related staff deployment delays. The civic body aims to collect the remaining ₹400 crore of its ₹900 crore annual revenue target, with over 14 lakh assessees. This initiative follows a successful early payment incentive scheme last year.

news.title: Karnataka government approves Greater Bengaluru's ₹18,000 crore suburban township
news.para: Karnataka government has approved the Rs 18,000-crore Greater Bengaluru integrated suburban township project, a 7,481-acre 'work-live-play' initiative spanning Bengaluru South district. The state will guarantee Rs 7,500 crore from Hudco, with the remaining funds sourced from BDA and CA site monetization. Land acquisition compensation is set at over Rs 2 crore per acre or equivalent developed land.

news.title: Prayagraj: PDA allows multiple family members to buy flats under new housing norms
news.para: The Prayagraj Development Authority has introduced new rules allowing multiple family members to purchase separate homes. This policy aims to boost housing sales and clear unsold properties. The authority will also partner with brokers to renovate and sell vacant flats. These changes are expected to make housing more accessible and affordable for buyers in Prayagraj.

news.title: MHADA drops flat prices in Vikhroli to 7.5% owing to poor public response
news.para: Mhada has reduced prices by 7.5% for 1,221 flats in Vikhroli, effectively cutting rates by Rs 10 lakh per unit. This move comes after poor public response to its lotteries, as once affordable flats now match market prices. Buyers are opting for private builders offering better quality and amenities.

news.title: Builders urge Gujarat RERA to extend project deadlines by six months
news.para: Credai Ahmedabad has requested a six-month extension for housing projects from GujRERA, citing rising construction costs and supply disruptions due to the West Asia conflict. Developers are facing a 10-20% increase in project expenses and material shortages, impacting their ability to meet deadlines. The association seeks relief under force majeure provisions, similar to the Covid-19 period.

news.title: Bombay HC orders razing of 16-storey Ulhasnagar building over ‘fraudulent permissions’
news.para: The Bombay High Court upheld the Ulhasnagar Municipal Corporation's order to demolish the 16-storey "Jhalak Paradise" building. The court criticized the developer for fraudulent permissions and civic officials for failing to verify documents, deeming the approvals null and void due to significant DP road encroachments.

news.title: Mumbai records over 13,800 property registrations in April: Knight Frank
news.para: Mumbai recorded over 13,800 property registrations in April 2026, marking the strongest April performance in over a decade. Stamp duty collections crossed ₹1,100 crore, reflecting sustained end-user demand despite seasonal moderation.

news.title: ACC's net profit declines 11.03% in FY26
news.para: ACC, an Adani Cement company, saw its net profit drop by over 11% in the financial year 2025-26. However, its total income grew by more than 14%. The company achieved its highest ever sales volume and revenue in the last quarter. Expansion projects are set to add significant capacity soon.

news.title: RR Kabel's net profit rises 57.96% in FY26
news.para: RR Kabel's net profit surged by over 57 percent, reaching ₹492.21 crore for the fiscal year 2025-26. Total income also saw a substantial increase of 27.50 percent, amounting to ₹9,778.78 crore. The Wires & Cables business was a major contributor to this success.

news.title: Kajaria Ceramics' net profit rises 62.32% in FY26; approves ₹297 crore share buyback
news.para: Kajaria Ceramics announced a significant 62.32 percent jump in net profit for FY26, reaching ₹487 crore. Total income also saw a rise. The company is investing ₹210 crore to expand its manufacturing capacity in Andhra Pradesh. Additionally, a share buyback of up to ₹296.7 crore has been approved. A final dividend of ₹6 per share is recommended.

news.title: Bombay HC steps into Decathlon-Godrej clash over prime Chembur lease
news.para: The Bombay High Court has placed prime Chembur commercial space under judicial protection amid a lease dispute. The court questioned if Decathlon's lease was terminated for a more lucrative deal with Titan, appointing a Receiver to take possession. This move signals a significant intervention in commercial fairness.

news.title: Allahabad HC refuses to entertain plea on Sahara Shahar land lease
news.para: The Allahabad High Court has rejected a plea from Sahara India Commercial Corporation Limited, which aimed to overturn the Lucknow Municipal Corporation's decision to revoke the land lease for the Sahara Shahar complex. The court indicated that it is unable to intervene at this time due to pending matters in the Supreme Court.

news.title: Homebuilder Persimmon sees energy price-led cost inflation, holds 2026 delivery target
news.para: Persimmon faces rising costs from energy prices extending into next year.  However, the company maintains its home delivery forecast.  Robust sales growth is reported for the initial months of 2026.  In-house production offers resilience against material cost hikes.  Early signs of supply chain inflation are noted for late 2026 and 2027.

news.title: Smartworks Coworking Spaces posts net profit of ₹10.52 crore in FY26
news.para: Smartworks Coworking Spaces achieved a net profit of ₹10.52 crore and total income of ₹1,849.90 crore in FY26. The company expanded its operational portfolio to over 10 million sq ft.  In Q4 FY26, total income was ₹532.70 crore with a profit after tax of ₹16.62 crore.  The company ended FY26 with net cash and reduced its gross debt significantly.

news.title: Lift safety checks lag in Pune, Mumbai divisions as recent mishaps in Nashik sound alarm bells
news.para: Lift safety inspections are alarmingly low in Pune and parts of Mumbai, with only 25.62% compliance in Pune city. Recent fatal incidents in Nashik highlight the risks of missed mandatory annual checks. Officials cite staff shortages and increasing workloads as key challenges, despite state-level announcements of new inspector posts and improved procedures.

news.title: Dehradun administration cancels registration of 17 illegal homestays
news.para: Dehradun district administration has cancelled registrations of 17 homestays in Sahaspur and Raipur as part of 'Operation Safai' due to violations. Inspections revealed issues like lack of proper facilities, fire safety lapses, and unauthorized commercial use. This action aims to ensure homestays adhere to regulations and promote local culture.

news.title: Accenture leases over six lakh sq ft in Pune, plans GCC in long-term ₹325 crore deal
news.para: Accenture has secured a significant office space of over 600,000 sq ft in Pune. This new facility will house a global capability centre. The occupation will occur in two phases, concluding by June. This expansion is part of a larger strategy to enhance operational efficiency and consolidate teams.
